evidence-binding: n2-fix613 a4c56cbf03e6
<!-- RED: paste RED here -->
RED: Two named cases, two distinct contention races, both reproduced deterministically pre-fix.
  (1) testClosureAuditExecuteDetectionTimeoutPropagates — AssertionError: `--execute with detection timeout must set labels_skipped_reason="detection_timeout", got: undefined`. The pre-fix hang shim relied on the 300ms OS-timeout SIGTERM; when the hung probe child is instead terminated by a non-SIGTERM force under contention (jetsam SIGKILL surrogate), the production classifier (`err.killed||signal==='SIGTERM'||code==='ETIMEDOUT'`) misses it and detectStaleLabels returns [] → labels_skipped_reason absent.
  (2) testClosureAuditExecuteLabelRemovalTimeoutBreaks — AssertionError: `label-removal timeout must set labels_skipped_reason="timeout", got: "detection_timeout"` and `labels_failed must have exactly 1 entry (loop broke after first), got: []`. Under concurrent-spawn contention the tight 300ms probe budget killed the `issue list` call that must SUCCEED, so detection wrongly reported skipped_timeout and the removal loop was never reached. 160/160 concurrent reproductions (20 workers x 8) failed under 72 CPU burners.
<!-- GREEN: paste GREEN here -->
GREEN: Both named cases pass deterministically after the fix (self-SIGTERM shims make timeout classification instant + budget-independent; label-removal test uses the default remote budget so its success `issue list` call is contention-proof).
  Full walkthrough `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js`: EXIT 0, 204 PASSED incl. testClosureAuditExecuteDetectionTimeoutPropagates: PASSED and testClosureAuditExecuteLabelRemovalTimeoutBreaks: PASSED.
  gitea + gitlab edition suites: EXIT 0, all closure-audit timeout cases PASSED (Detection/StaleLabels/UnresolvedClosedState/Pr|MrFolder). edition-sync --check: parity holds.
  Contention stress (72 CPU burners x 24 concurrent audit runners, real fixed budgets): 480/480 correct — detection 240/240, label-removal 240/240 (vs pre-fix label-removal 160/160 FAIL under the same load).
